overview.brief_description: Cardiff, the vibrant capital of Wales, blends rich history with modern culture along the scenic Bristol Channel. Known for its striking castle, thriving arts scene, and passionate rugby fans, the city offers a dynamic experience for every traveler.
why_visit: Experience world-class rugby at the Principality Stadium
Discover the vibrant Cardiff Bay waterfront
Enjoy diverse shopping, dining, and cultural events
overview.best_time_to_visit: May to September, when the weather is milder and outdoor festivals and events are in full swing

seasonal_notes.spring: Mild weather with blooming parks and gardens, perfect for sightseeing and outdoor walks.
seasonal_notes.summer: Warmest months with lively festivals, open-air markets, and vibrant nightlife.
seasonal_notes.autumn: Cooler temperatures with beautiful fall foliage and fewer tourists, ideal for cultural exploration.
seasonal_notes.winter: Chilly and occasionally rainy, but festive Christmas markets and cozy pubs provide seasonal charm.

practical_info.language: English (Welsh widely spoken and visible on signage)
practical_info.visit_duration: 2-3 days to cover main attractions comfortably
practical_info.getting_around: Efficient bus network, local trains, walking for city center, and taxis
practical_info.safety: Generally safe with standard urban precautions; stay alert in busy nightlife areas

must_do: Tour Cardiff Castle and its medieval walls
Watch a rugby match at Principality Stadium
Stroll through the rejuvenated Cardiff Bay and Mermaid Quay
Visit the National Museum Cardiff for art and natural history
Explore the shops and eateries on Queen Street
worth_doing: Relax in Bute Park’s green spaces
Discover St Fagans National Museum of History on the city outskirts
Visit Llandaff Cathedral, an architectural gem
Experience the nightlife in the arcades and Cardiff’s pubs
Take a boat tour around Cardiff Bay
hidden_gems: Explore the vintage shops and cafes in the Roath neighborhood
Visit the Norwegian Church Arts Centre, a peaceful cultural spot
Walk along the Taff Trail for scenic riverside views
Check out local art at the Chapter Arts Centre
Discover the secret gardens of Thompson’s Park

sites.name: Cardiff Castle
sites.description: Cardiff Castle is a medieval castle and Victorian Gothic mansion located in the city center. With Roman and Norman origins, it provides a fascinating glimpse into Cardiff's layered history. The interiors feature lavishly decorated rooms, and the castle grounds include beautiful gardens.
sites.seasonal_notes: Open year-round, but gardens are best in spring and summer
sites.booking_tips: Advance booking recommended during summer and holidays

opening_hours.notes: May close early on some holidays

visit_profile.weather_fallback: Indoor castle rooms and museum exhibits

sites.name: National Museum Cardiff
sites.description: The National Museum Cardiff hosts an extensive collection of art, natural history, and archaeology. Notable for its impressive collection of Impressionist paintings and Welsh craftwork, it offers free entry and engaging exhibitions.
sites.seasonal_notes: Open year-round; special exhibitions rotate seasonally
sites.booking_tips: No booking required for general admission; book ahead for special exhibitions

sites.name: Bute Park
sites.description: Bute Park is a vast green space adjacent to Cardiff Castle, offering riverside walks, formal gardens, and woodlands. It’s an urban oasis perfect for picnics, jogging, or simply relaxing, and hosts seasonal events throughout the year.
sites.seasonal_notes: Best experienced in spring and summer when flowers bloom

sites.name: Cardiff Bay
sites.description: Cardiff Bay is a vibrant waterfront area featuring modern architecture, restaurants, bars, and cultural venues. It’s home to the Wales Millennium Centre and offers boat tours and scenic walking routes along the water.
sites.seasonal_notes: Popular in summer; outdoor events often take place here
sites.booking_tips: Book boat tours in advance during peak season

sites.name: Wales Millennium Centre
sites.description: An iconic performing arts center famous for its striking design and cultural significance. It hosts opera, ballet, theater, and concerts, showcasing Welsh and international talent.
sites.seasonal_notes: Schedule varies seasonally; check calendar for events
sites.booking_tips: Book tickets in advance for popular shows

sites.name: St. Fagans National Museum of History
sites.description: An open-air museum showcasing Welsh life through reconstructed historic buildings and live demonstrations. It’s one of Europe's leading open-air museums and a fantastic place to explore Welsh culture and history.
sites.seasonal_notes: Best visited in spring and summer for outdoor activities
sites.booking_tips: No booking necessary for general admission but special events may require tickets

sites.name: Llandaff Cathedral
sites.description: A stunning medieval cathedral located in the historic suburb of Llandaff. Known for its beautiful stained glass windows, stone carvings, and peaceful grounds, it offers insight into Welsh ecclesiastical history.
sites.seasonal_notes: Open year-round; special services during holidays
sites.booking_tips: Free entry, donations suggested; guided tours available upon request

opening_hours.notes: Service times may restrict visitor access

sites.name: Roath Park
sites.description: Roath Park is a large Edwardian park featuring a boating lake, rose gardens, and a conservatory of exotic plants. It's a favorite spot for families and bird watchers, with a peaceful atmosphere away from the city bustle.
sites.seasonal_notes: Boating lake open in warmer months; gardens bloom in spring and summer

sites.name: The Principality Stadium
sites.description: Wales’ iconic national stadium hosts rugby and football matches as well as concerts. Visitors can tour the stadium to explore the players’ changing rooms, pitchside, and the Hall of Fame.
sites.seasonal_notes: Tours available year-round; event days may restrict access
sites.booking_tips: Pre-book tours online; buy event tickets in advance

sites.name: Chapter Arts Centre
sites.description: A contemporary arts hub offering cinema screenings, live performances, art exhibitions, and workshops. Known for its creative programming and vibrant café, it’s great for an artsy afternoon or evening.
sites.booking_tips: Check event schedules and book tickets online for popular shows

sites.name: Cardiff Market
sites.description: Located in the city center, Cardiff Market is a bustling Victorian indoor market offering fresh produce, Welsh delicacies, crafts, and street food. It’s a perfect spot to sample local flavors and mingle with locals.
sites.seasonal_notes: Open year-round; busier on weekends and holidays

sites.name: Sherman Theatre
sites.description: A leading venue for contemporary Welsh theatre and new writing, the Sherman Theatre offers a diverse program of plays and community events. It’s a cultural staple located opposite Cardiff Castle.
sites.seasonal_notes: Programs change seasonally; some shows may sell out

sites.name: Techniquest
sites.description: Techniquest is an interactive science discovery center ideal for families and curious minds. Exhibits cover physics, biology, and technology with hands-on displays and live demonstrations.
sites.seasonal_notes: Open year-round; special workshops during school holidays
sites.booking_tips: Book tickets online for discounts and busy periods

sites.name: The Coal Exchange
sites.description: A Grade I listed Victorian building once central to Cardiff’s coal trade. Now restored as an event venue and restaurant, it showcases impressive architecture and offers insight into the city’s industrial heritage.
sites.seasonal_notes: Open year-round for dining and events
sites.booking_tips: Reservations recommended for dining and special events

sites.name: Llandaff Fields
sites.description: A spacious public park offering sports facilities, playgrounds, and open green spaces for relaxation or exercise. It’s a popular local spot near the Llandaff area, perfect for a quiet stroll or picnic.
sites.seasonal_notes: Great for spring and summer visits

sites.name: Mount Stuart Square
sites.description: A beautifully restored historic dockside square in Cardiff Bay known for its colorful architecture and lively pubs and restaurants. It’s an excellent place to soak in local atmosphere and waterfront views.
sites.seasonal_notes: Popular in warmer months and evenings
sites.booking_tips: No booking needed for public square; reservations recommended at restaurants

sites.name: St John the Baptist Church
sites.description: One of Cardiff’s oldest churches dating back to the 12th century, St John the Baptist features medieval architecture and a calm interior. It’s located near the city center, offering a quiet historic retreat.
sites.seasonal_notes: Open year-round; special services on religious holidays

sites.name: Morgan Arcade
sites.description: A Victorian-era shopping arcade in Cardiff city center housing independent boutiques, cafes, and artisan shops. It offers a charming shopping experience away from mainstream retail outlets.
sites.seasonal_notes: Open year-round; festive decorations during holidays

neighborhoods.name: Cardiff City Centre
neighborhoods.character: The bustling heart of Cardiff pulses with energy, combining modern shopping and entertainment with historical landmarks. It's a vibrant hub where tourists and locals converge amid busy streets and iconic architecture.

location.description: Located at the geographic and cultural center of Cardiff

history.overview: Cardiff City Centre has evolved from a Roman fort to a medieval market town and now a modern capital city hub.
history.significance: It is the focal point for commerce, culture, and administration in Cardiff.

practical_info.safety: Generally safe with busy streets and visible policing
practical_info.best_time_to_visit: Spring to early autumn for festivals and shopping
practical_info.walkability: Highly walkable with pedestrian zones and public squares

attractions.name: Cardiff Castle
attractions.description: A medieval castle with Roman roots located in the city center.
attractions.appeal: Explore centuries of history and stunning architecture.

attractions.name: Principality Stadium
attractions.description: Home of Welsh rugby and major events with a retractable roof.
attractions.appeal: Experience thrilling sports and concerts in a world-class venue.

dining.name: The Clink Restaurant
dining.known_for: Innovative dishes prepared by rehabilitating prisoners

insider_tips: Visit early morning to enjoy quieter castle tours before crowds arrive.
Check event schedules at Principality Stadium to catch a rugby match or concert.
Use the pedestrianized streets to explore shops without traffic noise.

neighborhoods.name: Pontcanna
neighborhoods.character: Pontcanna is an affluent, leafy neighborhood known for its artistic flair and relaxed vibe. Quaint cafes, independent galleries, and boutique shops line its charming streets, attracting creatives and young professionals alike.

location.description: West of the city center, adjacent to the River Taff

history.overview: Originally a Victorian suburb with grand villas, Pontcanna has maintained its garden suburb character.
history.significance: It represents Cardiff’s artistic community and green urban living.
notable_events: Development as a residential area in late 19th century
Establishment of Chapter Arts Centre in 1971

transit: Frequent bus routes to city center
practical_info.safety: Very safe with low crime rates
practical_info.best_time_to_visit: Spring and summer for outdoor café culture
practical_info.walkability: Highly walkable with tree-lined streets and parks

attractions.name: Chapter Arts Centre
attractions.description: A hub for contemporary arts, cinema, and performances.
attractions.appeal: Discover independent films, exhibitions, and creative workshops.

attractions.name: Pontcanna Fields
attractions.description: Expansive green space along the River Taff ideal for walks and picnics.
attractions.appeal: Enjoy nature and outdoor activities close to the city.

dining.name: The Potted Pig
dining.known_for: Locally sourced meats and craft cocktails

insider_tips: Visit Chapter Arts Centre on weekends for live music and film screenings.
Walk along the River Taff for peaceful riverside views.
Try brunch at one of the independent cafes on Cathedral Road.

neighborhoods.name: Cardiff Bay
neighborhoods.character: Cardiff Bay is a vibrant waterfront district blending modern architecture with lively dining and entertainment. It buzzes with families, tourists, and nightlife seekers enjoying marina views and cultural venues.

location.description: South of the city center along the bay and waterfront

history.overview: Once an industrial dockland, Cardiff Bay was transformed in the late 20th century into a leisure and cultural hotspot.
history.significance: It symbolizes Cardiff’s post-industrial regeneration and maritime heritage.
notable_events: Opening of Wales Millennium Centre in 2004

practical_info.best_time_to_visit: Summer months for outdoor activities and festivals

attractions.name: Wales Millennium Centre
attractions.description: Iconic arts center hosting opera, theatre, and concerts.
attractions.appeal: Experience world-class performances in a striking building.

attractions.name: Techniquest
attractions.description: Interactive exhibits designed to engage all ages in science.
attractions.appeal: Fun and educational for families and curious visitors.

dining.name: The Jetty
dining.known_for: Fresh seafood with panoramic bay views

insider_tips: Take a boat tour from the marina to explore Cardiff Bay by water.
Visit Mermaid Quay in the evening for lively bars and music.
Check the Wales Millennium Centre schedule for free outdoor events during summer.

neighborhoods.name: Canton
neighborhoods.character: Canton is a vibrant, multicultural neighborhood blending working-class roots with modern regeneration. It offers a lively mix of independent shops, ethnic eateries, and community parks.

location.description: West of the city center, north of Grangetown

history.overview: Historically a working-class district linked to Cardiff’s industrial growth.
history.significance: Canton remains a stronghold of local culture with ongoing regeneration.

transit: Frequent bus routes to city center and Cardiff Bay
practical_info.safety: Moderate safety; usual city precautions advised
practical_info.best_time_to_visit: Late spring through early autumn for outdoor markets
practical_info.walkability: Walkable streets with local shops and parks

attractions.name: Victoria Park
attractions.description: A spacious green park popular for recreation and community events.
attractions.appeal: Ideal for picnics, sports, and seasonal fairs.

dining.name: Lemongrass & Ginger
dining.known_for: Authentic Thai flavors and cozy setting

dining.name: The Canton Club
dining.known_for: Craft beers and hearty British dishes

insider_tips: Explore the Saturday markets for local produce and crafts.
Try the diverse ethnic eateries along Cowbridge Road West.
Visit Victoria Park on weekends for community events and live music.

neighborhoods.name: Roath
neighborhoods.character: Roath is a lively, diverse neighborhood popular with students and young families. It balances Victorian terraces with green spaces and a strong community feel, known for its independent shops and cafés.

history.overview: Developed in the late 19th century as a residential suburb for the growing city.
history.significance: Roath has maintained its community spirit while adapting to student and family life.

transit: Bus routes connecting to city center
practical_info.safety: Generally safe with active neighborhood watch
practical_info.best_time_to_visit: Late spring to early autumn for park activities
practical_info.walkability: Highly walkable with tree-lined streets and local shops

attractions.name: Roath Park
attractions.description: A large park featuring a boating lake, rose gardens, and a conservatory.
attractions.appeal: Perfect for outdoor leisure and family outings.

attractions.name: The Globe Cinema
attractions.description: A community-run cinema showing independent and classic films.
attractions.appeal: Offers a cozy alternative to mainstream cinemas.

insider_tips: Rent a pedal boat on Roath Park lake during warmer months.
Catch an independent film screening at The Globe Cinema for a unique experience.
Visit the local markets on weekends to support independent vendors.

neighborhoods.name: Cathays
neighborhoods.character: Cathays is a student-centric neighborhood buzzing with youthful energy and diverse cultures. Its streets are filled with affordable eateries, vibrant pubs, and busy shops catering to university life.

location.description: Directly north of Cardiff City Centre

history.overview: Cathays grew rapidly in the 19th and 20th centuries as a residential area for workers and later students.
history.significance: It forms the educational and youthful backbone of the city.

transit: Frequent buses to city center and university campus
practical_info.best_time_to_visit: University term times for vibrant atmosphere
practical_info.walkability: Highly walkable with dense urban layout

attractions.name: Cardiff University Main Building
attractions.description: Historic and modern university buildings at the heart of Cathays.

insider_tips: Explore Cathays Cemetery for a quiet walk with Victorian monuments.
Visit local pubs on Crwys Road for student-friendly nightlife.
Try student-favorite eateries for affordable and tasty meals.

fine_dining.name: The Potted Pig
fine_dining.description: Set in a former bank vault, The Potted Pig offers contemporary British dishes with a focus on local Welsh produce and an extensive gin selection.
fine_dining.atmosphere: Chic and intimate with rustic industrial touches
fine_dining.local_tips: Try their signature Welsh lamb shoulder, slow-cooked to perfection.

fine_dining.name: Restaurant James Sommerin
fine_dining.description: Michelin-starred restaurant offering innovative dishes that emphasize seasonal Welsh ingredients in a refined setting overlooking Cardiff Bay.
fine_dining.local_tips: Book the tasting menu with wine pairing for a full experience.

fine_dining.name: Chapel 1877
fine_dining.description: Housed in a converted chapel, this stylish restaurant offers a seasonal menu focusing on Welsh ingredients with creative twists.
fine_dining.atmosphere: Trendy yet cozy with historic charm
fine_dining.local_tips: Try the Welsh beef dishes paired with local ales.

casual_dining.name: The Grazing Shed
casual_dining.description: Popular spot for inventive burger creations made with locally sourced Welsh beef and fresh ingredients.
casual_dining.local_tips: Try the 'Pigs in Blankets' burger, a local favorite.

casual_dining.name: Bar 44
casual_dining.description: Authentic tapas bar serving a broad selection of small plates and Spanish wines in a vibrant setting.
casual_dining.local_tips: Reserve early for weekend evenings to avoid waiting.

casual_dining.name: BrewDog Cardiff
casual_dining.description: Trendy craft beer bar with a strong focus on quality brews and elevated pub classics.
casual_dining.local_tips: Try their rotating taps featuring local Welsh craft beers.

casual_dining.name: The Plan
casual_dining.description: A cozy neighborhood bistro serving seasonal dishes with a focus on Welsh ingredients and natural wines.
casual_dining.local_tips: Their Sunday roast is a local favorite.

casual_dining.name: Sosban & The Old Butcher
casual_dining.description: Combines traditional Welsh recipes with modern techniques in a rustic, welcoming environment.
casual_dining.local_tips: Don't miss their homemade Welsh cakes for dessert.

street_food.name: Cardiff Street Food Market
street_food.description: Rotating street food vendors offering diverse dishes from around the world, held regularly at the Cardiff Market area.
street_food.local_tips: Arrive early on weekends for the best selection.

street_food.name: Thursdays at The Coal Exchange
street_food.description: Weekly event featuring top street food vendors alongside live music and craft drinks.
street_food.local_tips: Try the Welsh rarebit stand for a local twist.

street_food.name: The Pumphouse Market
street_food.description: Pop-up market focusing on Welsh artisan producers and street food vendors, perfect for tasting local specialties.
street_food.local_tips: Sample locally made cheeses and ciders.

bars.name: The Dead Canary
bars.description: Award-winning craft cocktail bar known for inventive drinks and speakeasy vibe.
bars.local_tips: Ask the bartenders for custom cocktails tailored to your taste.

bars.name: Tiny Rebel Cardiff
bars.description: Popular brewery taproom offering a wide range of award-winning Welsh craft beers with a relaxed vibe.
bars.local_tips: Try their flagship Cwtch red ale.

bars.name: Lab 22
bars.description: Trendy bar with creative cocktails and modern bar snacks in a vibrant setting.
bars.local_tips: Try their seasonal cocktail menu featuring local ingredients.

cafes.name: Coffee Barker
cafes.description: Known for expertly brewed coffee and a minimalist, welcoming atmosphere, perfect for coffee aficionados.

cafes.name: Brew Lab
cafes.description: Artisanal coffee roasters and café offering rich blends and delicious pastries in a bright setting.
cafes.local_tips: Their house blend espresso is a must-try.

cafes.name: Good Mood Food
cafes.description: Vibrant café focusing on nutritious, plant-based dishes alongside excellent coffee.
cafes.local_tips: Try the avocado toast with a twist of Welsh sea salt.

food_experiences.name: Cardiff Food Tour
food_experiences.description: Guided walking tour sampling Cardiff’s signature dishes, local produce, and hidden culinary gems across the city centre.
food_experiences.local_tips: Book in advance, tours often include tasting Welsh rarebit and laverbread.

food_experiences.name: Welsh Cooking Class at The Chef's Table
food_experiences.description: Hands-on cooking class focusing on traditional Welsh dishes using local ingredients, including cawl and Welsh cakes.
food_experiences.local_tips: Perfect for food lovers wanting to bring Welsh flavors home.

food_experiences.name: Cardiff Market Visit & Tasting
food_experiences.description: Explore the historic Cardiff Market with a local guide, tasting fresh Welsh cheeses, meats, and artisanal products.
food_experiences.local_tips: Visit early for the freshest produce and best vendor interaction.

food_experiences.name: Welsh Gin Distillery Tour
food_experiences.description: Tour a local gin distillery near Cardiff, learning about the production process and tasting a range of Welsh gins.
food_experiences.local_tips: Combine with a craft cocktail masterclass for a full experience.

spring: Lamb dishes featuring tender spring lamb
Stinging nettle soup – a traditional seasonal Welsh dish
summer: Welsh strawberries, often served with cream
Fresh seafood from Cardiff Bay including crab and mussels
Cold Welsh cider and craft beers
autumn: Game meats such as venison and pheasant
Mushroom foraging dishes featuring chanterelles and porcini
winter: Hearty Welsh cawl (lamb and vegetable stew)
Welsh cakes served warm with tea

transport_types.notes: Direct trains from Cardiff Central to London Paddington.
Budget option with several departures daily.
Direct flights from Cardiff Airport to Paris Charles de Gaulle.
Train from Cardiff to London, then Eurostar to Paris.
Direct flights from Cardiff Airport to Amsterdam Schiphol.

transport_types.operator: Great Western Railway + Eurostar + NS Dutch Railways
transport_types.notes: Train via London and Brussels with transfers.
Direct flights from Cardiff Airport to Dublin Airport.
Ferry from Holyhead (accessible by train from Cardiff) to Dublin Port.
Train from Cardiff to London, then Eurostar to Brussels Midi.
Connecting flights via London or direct flights vary seasonally.
Connecting flights via London Heathrow or direct seasonal flights.
Direct flights from Cardiff Airport to Barcelona El Prat.
Direct and indirect train options available.
Overnight buses connecting Cardiff and Edinburgh.
Connecting flights via London or direct seasonal flights.
Train via London St Pancras with Eurostar to Lille Europe.

Spring.name: Cardiff Half Marathon
Spring.description: A popular running event attracting thousands, showcasing the city's landmarks and vibrant atmosphere.

Spring.name: Cardiff Spring Food Festival
Spring.description: Celebrate local produce and seasonal Welsh delicacies with stalls, tastings, and cooking demonstrations.

Spring.name: Bute Park Walks and Blossom Viewing
Spring.description: Enjoy peaceful walks among blooming cherry blossoms and fresh spring greenery in one of Cardiff's largest parks.

Spring.name: St David’s Day Celebrations
Spring.description: Join parades, concerts, and cultural events celebrating Wales’ patron saint with traditional music and dance.

Summer.name: Cardiff International Food and Drink Festival
Summer.description: A lively festival offering a wide range of street food, craft beers, and live music in a family-friendly setting.

Summer.name: Outdoor Cinema at Cardiff Castle
Summer.description: Experience classic and contemporary films in the stunning open-air setting of Cardiff Castle’s grounds.

Summer.name: Cardiff Bay Water Sports
Summer.description: Enjoy kayaking, paddleboarding, or sailing in the calm waters of Cardiff Bay during warm summer months.

Summer.name: Roald Dahl Plass Summer Market
Summer.description: Browse a variety of fresh, local produce and artisan goods at this weekly summer market by the waterfront.

Summer.name: Bute Park Summer Family Fun
Summer.description: Participate in family-friendly events and open-air games set within the lush surroundings of Bute Park.

Autumn.name: Cardiff International Food and Drink Festival (Autumn Edition)
Autumn.description: Discover seasonal Welsh produce and warming dishes as the festival returns with a cozy autumnal atmosphere.

Autumn.name: Autumn Walks in Roath Park
Autumn.description: Enjoy colorful foliage and crisp air while strolling around Roath Park’s lake and botanical gardens.

Autumn.name: Cardiff Literature Festival
Autumn.description: Attend author talks, workshops, and readings celebrating Welsh and international literature in various venues.
Autumn.location: Chapter Arts Centre and city venues

Autumn.name: Welsh Rarebit and Cider Tastings
Autumn.description: Sample traditional Welsh rarebit paired with locally crafted ciders, a warming treat for cooler days.

Autumn.name: Cardiff Bay Autumn Photography Walks
Autumn.description: Capture the changing light and vibrant colors of the bay area during guided or self-led photography walks.

Winter.name: Cardiff Winter Wonderland and Christmas Market
Winter.description: Enjoy festive stalls, ice skating, rides, and seasonal treats in the heart of Cardiff during the holiday season.

Winter.name: Pantomime at New Theatre Cardiff
Winter.description: Experience the traditional British pantomime with family-friendly performances full of humor and music.

Winter.name: Winter Walks in Bute Park
Winter.description: Take brisk walks through frosty paths and enjoy the serene winter landscape of Bute Park.

Winter.name: Welsh Winter Warmers – Cawl and Bara Brith
Winter.description: Taste traditional Welsh winter dishes like hearty cawl stew and the spiced fruit bread bara brith in local cafés.

Winter.name: Cardiff Bay New Year’s Fireworks
Winter.description: Welcome the new year with spectacular fireworks over Cardiff Bay accompanied by live music and celebrations.

ranges.notes: Cold and wet weather; New Year events bring moderate crowds, but overall tourist activity is low.
Cold, damp, and often grey weather; very quiet period with few events or tourists.
Weather remains chilly and wet; some indoor cultural activities available but low tourist traffic.
Typically grey and cold with occasional rain; good for budget travelers seeking low crowds and cheap accommodation.
Cold and wet weather persists; some local indoor events but generally quiet for tourists.
Valentine's Day brings special events and dining options; weather still chilly but some romantic appeal for couples.
Weather remains cold but slightly improving; indoor cultural events and museums offer good options; crowd levels remain low.
Early March can still be chilly and wet, but days lengthen and some signs of spring emerge; moderate crowd levels.
Welsh national day festivities boost local culture and events; weather improves somewhat; good time for cultural travelers.
Late March sees milder weather and fewer crowds than spring peak; good for outdoor activities and exploring city parks.
Spring weather improves with mild temperatures; food festival attracts visitors; moderate crowds and lively atmosphere.
Pleasant spring weather; ideal for outdoor sightseeing in parks and Cardiff Bay; low to moderate crowd levels.
Easter attracts families and tourists; good weather usually; higher crowd levels and prices but many cultural and family events.
Mild and often sunny weather; outdoor events and festivals start; moderate to high crowds around holidays.
One of the best months for weather with warm, sunny days; ideal for outdoor activities and sightseeing; low to moderate crowds.
Late May remains mild and pleasant; some events start leading into summer; crowds growing but still manageable.
Early summer with warm weather and long daylight hours; excellent for outdoor sightseeing and events; moderate crowds.

ranges.event: Cardiff International Food & Drink Festival (mid June)
ranges.notes: Food festival boosts local culture and tourism; warm weather and vibrant atmosphere; good for all traveler types.
Warm and sunny weather continues; ideal for exploring Cardiff Bay and outdoor festivals; moderate to high crowds.

ranges.event: Cardiff International Food & Drink Festival continues (early July)
ranges.notes: Peak summer weather with warm temperatures; food festival and cultural events; high tourist crowds and lively atmosphere.
Warmest month with long days; ideal for outdoor activities, festivals, and city tours; very high crowd levels and accommodation prices.
Continued warm and dry weather; peak tourism season; busy but vibrant city life; book accommodations early.
Summer festival season continues with Cardiff Carnival; warm weather; very high crowds and lively atmosphere.
Warm but occasionally rainy; summer crowds remain high but start to slightly decline after mid-August; good for families and outdoor activities.
Late August sees crowd levels decrease slightly; weather still warm; good for sightseeing and events with fewer tourists.
Early autumn with mild weather and fewer crowds; ideal for budget travelers seeking good weather and lower prices.

ranges.event: Cardiff International Food & Drink Festival (late September)
ranges.notes: Food festival and cooler but pleasant weather; lower crowds than summer; good for cultural tourists and families.
Autumn weather cools and becomes wetter; fewer tourists; good for indoor cultural activities but outdoor sightseeing less pleasant.
Cooler temperatures and some rain; Halloween events attract moderate crowds; good for families and couples interested in seasonal festivities.
Cold, wet and often grey weather; low tourist numbers; good for budget travelers but limited outdoor activities.
Chilly and damp with short daylight hours; few events; low crowds and low prices but weather may deter some travelers.
Cold and wet but festive atmosphere begins; Christmas markets start attracting visitors; moderate crowds in weekends.

ranges.event: Cardiff Christmas Market and Winter Wonderland
ranges.notes: Festive season in full swing with markets, events, and decorations; cold weather but vibrant atmosphere; high crowds on weekends.
Major holidays with festive events and celebrations; cold and often wet; high crowds especially on Christmas Eve and New Year's Eve.
